beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and grab coffee at home.
beats.reasoning: Monday morning routine; full energy to start the week. Coffee at home before heading to work.

beats.activity: Drive to Redmond for morning clinic shift.
beats.reasoning: Across-town commute from Madison Park to Redmond; budgeted 50 min for typical Monday traffic on I-90.

beats.activity: Patient consultations and physical therapy sessions at the clinic.
beats.reasoning: Morning shift at sports clinic; Sebastian is disciplined and focused on client care before the 11am FIFA match.

beats.activity: Quick lunch break at clinic or nearby café.
beats.reasoning: Short break between morning shift and early afternoon; grabs something light before heading downtown.

beats.activity: Drive from Redmond to Downtown Seattle to catch the USA vs Wales match at a sports bar.
beats.reasoning: Sunny Monday + FIFA match at 11am: Sebastian heads downtown early enough to grab a seat at a bar near Pike Place Market (skipping the actual match but arriving for the postgame energy and drinks with locals).

beats.activity: Watch the tail end of the FIFA match and socialize at a sports bar downtown.
beats.location_name: The Crumpet Shop / Pike Place Market area (Downtown Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Sebastian can't resist the buzz of a World Cup match; he arrives after work and enjoys the social atmosphere and banter over beers with strangers—classic sarcastic Seattleite move.

beats.activity: Browse used bookstore and vintage shops in Capitol Hill.
beats.reasoning: Can't resist a bookstore; heads to Capitol Hill to hunt for thrifted finds and used books on a sunny afternoon—feeds his curiosity and love of treasure-hunting.

beats.activity: Thrifting and vintage shopping in Capitol Hill.
beats.reasoning: Perfect sunny afternoon for exploring thrift stores; Sebastian lingers longer than expected, hunting for vintage video game memorabilia or rare finds—feeds his gaming passion and curiosity.
Heads home as evening approaches; budgeted 35 min for Capitol Hill to Madison Park on a Monday evening with lighter traffic.

beats.activity: Cook dinner and relax at home.
beats.reasoning: Wind down after a full day; Sebastian cooks a simple dinner and settles in for the evening, reflecting on the match and his thrifting haul.

beats.activity: Video gaming and leisure time at home.
beats.reasoning: Perfect way to end a Monday; Sebastian unwinds with video games, staying in his comfort zone after a social afternoon downtown and an active day of work and thrifting.
Wind down complete; Sebastian gets a solid 7.5 hours of sleep after a full, engaging Monday—he'll wake refreshed for Tuesday.

diary: Woke up early to catch the World Cup match—couldn't resist slipping out of the clinic during lunch. The morning flew by with back-to-back patient sessions; one guy had a nasty rotator cuff issue that reminded me why I love this work. Grabbed a quick sandwich at Redmond Town Center, then made the drive downtown with the sun beating through the windshield. The sports bar was already packed by the time I arrived, and I caught the last bit of USA vs Wales with a beer and some genuinely good company. Pike Place felt alive today. After the match ended, I wandered over to Capitol Hill—hit up Horizon Books and spent way too long at Scarecrow Video flipping through old DVDs. Found a pristine copy of something I'd been hunting for months. Drove home feeling loose and satisfied, cooked a simple pasta dinner, and lost myself in gaming until the world got quiet. Good Monday.
thought_process: The World Cup timing made today feel spontaneous in a way work usually doesn't allow. I almost talked myself out of the drive downtown—logistics were tight—but the weather was perfect, and I haven't done something impulsive like that in weeks. Work felt grounding this morning; that patient's shoulder problem kept me engaged. The match break recharged me, honestly. Shopping in Capitol Hill wasn't planned; I just followed the impulse after the bar and ended up exactly where I needed to be. My mood shifted from routine-focused this morning to genuinely present by afternoon. The Light Rail closure didn't even register because I was driving anyway. By evening, I felt like I'd actually lived the day instead of just moving through it.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
